title: After
description: After Mordaine's defeat. The throne room flickers. A portal to World 2 opens. The party questions the nature of their journey.

lines: Mordaine dissolved. Not in the way enemies dissolve — in smoke and light and theatrical satisfaction.
She dissolved the way a thought dissolves. One moment she was there. The next, a memory already fading.
The throne room flickered. Not the lights — the room. The walls shuddered, and for a fraction of a second, the party saw something behind them.
Not stone. Not darkness. Something that looked like... suburbs.

steps.text: In Harmonia Village, a device that had appeared overnight hummed to life. Its portal shimmered with images of parking lots and picket fences.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Mordaine designed our journey. All of it. The cave. The forest. The Masterites. Us.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: She designed a challenge. We happened to be the ones inside it.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It's not worse either. The design is real. The challenges were real. What we built — the way we fight, the way we adapt — that came from us, not from her.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Does it count if everything we overcame was built to be overcome?

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Does any game count if the pieces were designed to fit together?

lines: The word landed strangely. Game. A children's word. A word for dice and boards and rules someone else wrote.
None of them had used it before.
They looked at the portal. Behind it, a world that made no sense. Ahead: the unknown.
The Fighter went first. Because that was what fighters were for.
